behavior_in_task_description.reason: instruction.md names the functions (imageAttachments, prompt.set, closePopover, clearEditor, setEditorText, focusEditorEnd, promptProbe.select, command.trigger, DEFAULT_PROMPT), the part types ('text', 'image_attachment'), the 'slash' source string, and the early-return behavior on undefined cmd — all of which the tests assert.

no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction describes what's broken and expected behavior (preserve image_attachments, include them in prompt.set) without specifying line numbers or exact code diffs.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests check counts of image_attachment parts in the prompt.set args and trigger arg values, not variable names or exact line positions; alternative orderings/implementations would still pass.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests extract handleSlashSelect, transpile, and execute it in a VM with mocks, plus run bun typecheck and repo unit tests.

test_not_tautological.reason: A stub returning nothing would fail because tests require promptSetCalled to be true with correct imageCount, and triggerArgs to equal [id, 'slash'].

behavior_in_tests.reason: Tests cover custom/builtin image preservation, undefined cmd early return, closePopover, and command.trigger('id','slash') — all behaviors described.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped with section comments ('Gates (pass_to_pass, static)', '[repo_tests] pass_to_pass') and descriptively named.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task modifies UI component state, not structured data output.

no_typos.reason: Paths and function names (packages/app/src/components/prompt-input.tsx, handleSlashSelect, imageAttachments) are consistent.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: instruction.md does not identify the file or line; it describes the component and functions generically.
